codeReview.summary: The rerun does not resolve the original review failure. The feature does expose root `sampleTest`/`allureReport` entrypoints and gives a clear pre-test message, but it still does not wire real Allure reporting: the report task writes placeholder HTML and the test task fabricates a plain-text result file instead of emitting Allure-compatible results.

issues.description: The root `allureReport` task is still a handwritten placeholder implementation. It writes `build/reports/allure-report/index.html` itself and only logs a file count (`build.gradle:37-78`) instead of invoking any actual Allure Gradle/plugin integration. Because the repository has no real Allure task wiring, the feature's requirement to "configure Allure reporting tasks" remains unmet.

`test-suite:test` still manufactures `build/allure-results/sample-test-result.txt` with ad-hoc `status/task/timestamp` text (`test-suite/build.gradle:24-31`) and labels it "Allure-compatible" in logs. That file is not an Allure result artifact, so even after test execution the root reporting entrypoint is consuming fake input rather than real Allure results.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The bootstrap worker skill still leaves too much ambiguity around reporting work. It tells workers to exercise `./gradlew allureReport --no-daemon`, but it never explicitly requires real Allure plugin/task integration or forbids placeholder report/result generation, which is how this contract-mismatched implementation was produced and then treated as successful.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /home/kratos/projects/e2e/.factory/skills/framework-bootstrap-worker/SKILL.md:36 requires exercising the pre-test `allureReport` path; commit `e4005c12a47af1d00a0e393a992efa7846307835` adds placeholder-only logic in `build.gradle:37-78` and `test-suite/build.gradle:14-31` instead of real Allure wiring.

summary: Review failed again: the current repository state still substitutes placeholder report/result generation for real Allure integration, so the feature does not satisfy its Allure reporting contract.
